author: assistant
conditions: Implementation must remain confined to the static sidecar contract, validator, directly necessary documentation, bounded fixtures, focused tests, and self-model records.
Canonical human-readable artifacts must remain the sole authoritative source; sidecars must be explicitly derived.
Every sidecar contract instance must include source identity, source path, source content hash, source repository commit, schema version, generator identity, and generation timestamp.

The contract must define purpose, status, key claims, decisions, results, constraints, risks, dependencies, evidence references, uncertainty, unresolved items, next actions, and authoritative lineage.
The contract must include explicit omission and truncation metadata and must not imply semantic completeness without fidelity evidence.
Rich immediate-neighbor and concise second-level profiles must remain distinct.
The approximate 250-500 and 80-200 token ranges remain hypotheses for validation and must not be treated as fixed production limits.
Validation must distinguish schema validity, provenance validity, profile compliance, staleness, and semantic fidelity.
Only synthetic or explicitly designated bounded fixtures may be created in this slice.
Do not generate corpus-wide, production, provider-produced, or model-produced sidecars.
Do not modify canonical Markdown or JSON artifact content.
Do not modify ContextPackManifest.items, graph_neighborhood_context_items, build_latest_context_manifest, graph packing, or token-budget enforcement.
Do not change scripts/build_context_pack.py, ask_provider.py, compare_providers.py, provider prompts, prompt rendering, or CLI behavior.
Do not add automatic target inference or automatic graph-local inclusion.
Do not add graph persistence, retrieval, embeddings, reranking, production index mutation, memory-store mutation, or runtime-manifest mutation.
GAP-0002 must remain open after this slice.
Any scope expansion beyond this warrant requires a separate proposed and admitted plan.
reason: PLAN-20260717-0001 is a bounded, contract-first response to CAP-0012 and EVAL-20260716-0001, which found usable semantic compact coverage for only 17 of 43 audited artifacts and recommended a separate compact representation contract. The plan keeps canonical human-readable artifacts authoritative, requires derived sidecars to carry provenance, source hashes, versions, omission metadata, staleness semantics, and fidelity evidence, and validates the contract only with bounded fixtures. No corpus-wide sidecar generation or context-selection behavior is admitted.
scope: Admit only the contract implementation described by PLAN-20260717-0001: a static compact artifact-sidecar schema and validator; deterministic identity, path, serialization, and ordering rules; rich immediate-neighbor and concise second-level profile definitions; provenance, source-hash, versioning, omission, staleness, regeneration, supersession, and deletion semantics; semantic fidelity validation categories; documentation; and synthetic or explicitly designated bounded fixtures. No bulk or production sidecars, provider/model generation, graph-packing integration, context selection, provider exposure, automatic inclusion, graph persistence, retrieval, embeddings, reranking, index mutation, memory-store mutation, runtime manifest mutation, or GAP-0002 closure is admitted.
